scope.summary:
Goal: Implement three navigation aids that port the React editor workbench chrome (MT-245 era) into the native editor: (1) a minimap panel on the right side of the editor showing a scaled-down view of the whole file with the current viewport highlighted, (2) an outline/heading tree panel (symbols extracted from tree-sitter: functions, classes, structs) with click-to-scroll, (3) a go-to-line palette command (Ctrl+G) that opens a numeric input and scrolls the editor. Expected diff shape: new `src/frontend/code_editor/minimap.rs`, new `src/frontend/code_editor/outline.rs`, edits to `src/frontend/code_editor/mod.rs` and `src/frontend/panels/code_editor_panel.rs`.

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/code_editor/minimap.rs` — `Minimap` struct. `Minimap::render(ui: &mut egui::Ui, buffer: &TextBuffer, highlight_spans: &[HighlightSpan], visible_range: Range<usize>, total_lines: usize)` draws a narrow panel (default width: 80px, configurable) with one pixel row per file line (or one per N lines if file is very large — use `max(1, total_lines / panel_height_px)` as the compression ratio). Each row is colored with the average highlight color of that line's spans (or a neutral gray for unhighlighted lines). Draws a semi-transparent viewport rect over the rows corresponding to `visible_range`. Click on the minimap scrolls the editor to that line. Hover shows a tooltip with the line number.
- CREATE `src/frontend/code_editor/outline.rs` — `OutlineProvider::compute(tree: &tree_sitter::Tree, buffer: &TextBuffer) -> Vec<OutlineItem>` where `OutlineItem { kind: OutlineKind, name: String, line: usize, indent: usize }`. `OutlineKind` enum: `Function`, `Method`, `Class`, `Struct`, `Enum`, `Constant`, `Module`. Extracts named identifiers from tree-sitter node types (e.g. for Rust: `function_item` -> name from child `identifier`, `impl_item` -> type name). `OutlinePanel::show(ui: &mut Ui, items: &[OutlineItem], on_navigate: impl FnOnce(usize))` renders a collapsible tree list; clicking an item calls `on_navigate(item.line)` to scroll the editor.
- EDIT `src/frontend/code_editor/mod.rs` — add `outline_items: Vec<OutlineItem>` (recomputed on tree re-parse), `show_outline: bool`, `show_minimap: bool`, `goto_line_state: Option<GotoLineState>` (where `GotoLineState { input: String, parsed: Option<usize> }`). Methods: `open_goto_line()` opens the palette; Ctrl+G triggers it.
- EDIT `src/frontend/panels/code_editor_panel.rs` — split the panel into a horizontal layout: `[outline panel (optional, left)] [editor area (center)] [minimap (optional, right)]`. The go-to-line palette renders as a modal overlay centered in the editor area (small `egui::Window`).

Exact crates/functions/APIs to use:
- `egui::Painter::rect_filled` — for minimap rows and viewport indicator.
- `egui::ScrollArea` — for the outline panel (outline can be long).
- `egui::Window` — for the go-to-line palette modal.
- `egui::CollapsingHeader` — for the outline tree hierarchy.
- AccessKit: `author_id = "code_editor_minimap"` `Role::ScrollBar` on the minimap (clicking scrolls); `author_id = "code_editor_outline"` `Role::Tree` on the outline panel; `author_id = "code_editor_goto_line"` `Role::TextInput` on the go-to-line input.

React reference files:
- `app/src/harness/editor_workbench_chrome_harness.tsx` — `ED-NAV-004` (prose outline panel heading tree + click-to-scroll) and `ED-NAV-006` (go-to-line inside code block from palette). The native implementation provides the same features for the code editor surface.
- `app/src/lib/editor/editor_chrome.ts` — `DocumentChromeStatus` and the status bar; the outline panel in the native editor is analogous to the heading tree in the rich editor.
- `app/src/harness/editor_workbench_chrome_harness.tsx` lines 9-10 — `ED-NAV-006: go-to-line inside the focused embedded code block (palette)`. Native equivalent is the go-to-line palette (Ctrl+G).

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Minimap: paint each row as a 1px-tall, 80px-wide rect. Color: `scope_to_color(dominant_scope_of_line, visuals)` if any highlight spans cover the line, else `visuals.window_fill()` darkened by 10%. For very large files (>5000 lines), draw one pixel per `ceil(total_lines / panel_height_px)` lines (group average color). Use `ui.input(|i| i.pointer.press_origin())` on the minimap Rect to detect click-to-scroll.
2. Outline: use the same `TreeCursor` as `FoldProvider` (potentially share a single tree traversal for both). Compute indent by tracking depth in the TreeCursor walk.
3. Go-to-line: open with Ctrl+G. The modal window has a single `TextEdit::singleline` pre-populated with the current cursor line. On Enter: parse the input as `usize`, subtract 1 for 0-indexing, clamp to `0..buffer.len_lines()`, set the editor scroll offset to `y_for_line(target_line)` and move the primary cursor to byte offset of that line.
4. Layout split: use `egui::SidePanel::left("outline").resizable(true).default_width(180)` for the outline and `egui::SidePanel::right("minimap").resizable(false).exact_width(80)` for the minimap. The center is the main editor ScrollArea.
5. Outline click-to-scroll: `on_navigate(line)` updates `CodeEditorPanel::scroll_to_line = Some(line)`. In the next `show()` call, if `scroll_to_line.is_some()`, compute the scroll offset and set it on the ScrollArea state via `scroll_area.scroll_to_row(...)`.

Dependencies/unblock conditions: MT-001 (tree-sitter tree), MT-002 (VirtualLineLayout and visible_range), MT-005 (fold region list needed to correctly compute visible line count for minimap).
Risk if missed: Navigation in large files is poor without minimap and outline — a significant operator workflow deficiency for code review tasks.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend outline_provider` passes — tests: parse 20-line Rust file with two functions, outline contains 2 OutlineItems of kind=Function with correct names and line numbers.
AC-002: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend goto_line` passes — tests: go-to-line with valid line, go-to-line clamped past end, go-to-line with non-numeric input (no crash, no navigation).
AC-003: egui_kittest screenshot shows three-panel layout: outline panel (left), editor area (center), minimap (right), all visible with correct widths.

AC-004: egui_kittest interactive test: outline panel renders at least 2 items for a Rust snippet; clicking item 1 navigates editor scroll to item 1's line (verified via AccessKit scroll position or screenshot).
AC-005: egui_kittest interactive test: Ctrl+G opens go-to-line modal (verified via AccessKit code_editor_goto_line node present); typing '5' + Enter scrolls editor to line 5.
AC-006: Minimap click at vertical midpoint scrolls editor to approximately the middle line of the buffer (within ±3 lines).
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend outline_provider` — GREEN, logged.
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend goto_line` — GREEN, logged.
PT-003: egui_kittest screenshot `test_output/MT-006-three-panel.png` saved and pixel-verified for three distinct panel regions.
PT-004: egui_kittest test `outline_navigate` — navigate to line, verify scroll.
PT-005: egui_kittest test `goto_line_modal` — AccessKit confirms modal open and navigation.
implementation_notes: Minimap rendering cost: for a 10k-line file at 80px panel height, we paint 80 rows of 80px each = 6400 rect calls. Use a single `egui::Shape::Vec` batch to reduce draw calls.

Outline reuse: share the tree traversal with FoldProvider — both walk the same tree. Consider a single `TreeAnalyzer::analyze(tree, buffer) -> TreeAnalysis { fold_regions, outline_items }` function called once per tree re-parse.
SidePanel memory: egui stores SidePanel state by Id. Use stable Ids: `egui::Id::new("code_editor_outline_panel")` and `egui::Id::new("code_editor_minimap_panel")`.
Minimap viewport indicator: use `egui::Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(255, 255, 255, 30)` overlay rect covering the visible_range rows.
ports_from_react: app/src/harness/editor_workbench_chrome_harness.tsx — ED-NAV-004 (outline heading tree with click-to-scroll) and ED-NAV-006 (go-to-line palette); these are the React reference behaviors to match.
app/src/lib/editor/editor_chrome.ts — DocumentChromeStatus pattern; the native editor emits equivalent status events for the swarm diagnostic surface.
binds_backend_api: None — minimap, outline, and go-to-line are purely in-process. Backend code-nav (symbol lookup) is wired in MT-008.

risks: RISK-001: Three-panel layout reduces the center editor area below usable width on small screens. Mitigate: make outline and minimap hideable via toggle buttons; persist toggle state in egui memory.
RISK-002: Outline and fold traversal run on the same tree in the same frame — two separate TreeCursor walks. Mitigate: unify into a single `TreeAnalyzer` struct called once per buffer_version tick.
RISK-003: Go-to-line parse with user input '0' or negative — must clamp to line 0 without crashing. Mitigate: parse as i64, clamp to 1..=buffer.len_lines(), subtract 1.
RISK-004: Minimap hover tooltip creates a new egui tooltip widget every frame on hover — can accumulate if not properly managed. Mitigate: use `egui::Response::on_hover_text()` which is idempotent.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Three-panel layout must have toggle buttons for outline and minimap — verified in screenshot AC-003.
MC-002: Unified TreeAnalyzer recommended — document in a code comment that FoldProvider and OutlineProvider share the same traversal.
MC-003: Go-to-line input must clamp and not panic — verified in AC-002.
